events.title: Whiskey Rebellion
events.summary: Washington personally led federalized militia forces to suppress the Whiskey Rebellion, asserting federal authority over violent tax resistance and preventing the young republic from fragmenting under domestic insurrection.
sources: National Archives, Whiskey Rebellion correspondence.
Library of Congress, Washington domestic crisis papers.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Washington managed the first major domestic uprising by combining force with measured clemency, cementing federal supremacy in crises.
sources: Library of Congress, Washington presidential papers.
National Archives, early federal crisis records.

events.title: Quasi‑War with France
events.summary: Adams expanded the Navy and authorized limited naval conflict with France while resisting full‑scale war, balancing national honor with the risk of escalation.
sources: National Archives, Adams naval and diplomatic records.
Library of Congress, Quasi‑War correspondence.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Adams managed an undeclared naval war by strengthening defenses yet avoiding a broader conflict that could have destabilized the young republic.
sources: Library of Congress, Adams presidential papers.
National Archives, early foreign crisis records.

events.title: Barbary Pirates Conflict
events.summary: Jefferson deployed naval forces against the Barbary states to end tribute payments, managing a maritime security crisis without a large standing army.
sources: National Archives, Barbary War naval records.
Library of Congress, Jefferson foreign crisis papers.

events.title: Embargo Crisis of 1807
events.summary: Jefferson imposed a sweeping embargo to avoid war with Britain and France, triggering severe domestic economic backlash and widespread evasion.
sources: Library of Congress, Jefferson embargo correspondence.
National Archives, embargo enforcement records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Jefferson used naval force abroad and sweeping embargo at home, avoiding war but creating a major self‑inflicted economic crisis.
sources: Library of Congress, Jefferson presidential papers.
National Archives, early crisis management records.

events.title: War of 1812 and Burning of Washington
events.summary: Madison led the nation through the War of 1812, including the British burning of Washington, relying on militia, limited federal capacity, and eventual diplomatic settlement.
sources: National Archives, War of 1812 records.
Library of Congress, Madison wartime correspondence.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Madison’s crisis management was marked by early military failures but eventual survival of the republic and affirmation of U.S. independence.
sources: Library of Congress, Madison presidential papers.
National Archives, wartime crisis records.

events.title: Panic of 1819
events.summary: Monroe presided over the first major U.S. peacetime financial crisis, responding with limited federal intervention and leaving most relief to states and private actors.
sources: National Archives, Panic of 1819 fiscal records.
Library of Congress, Monroe economic crisis papers.

events.title: Missouri Crisis
events.summary: Monroe oversaw the political crisis over Missouri’s admission, accepting the Missouri Compromise line to avert immediate sectional rupture.
sources: Library of Congress, Monroe Missouri correspondence.
National Archives, Missouri statehood records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Monroe managed early financial and sectional crises with minimal federal economic action but acceptance of a fragile political compromise.
sources: Library of Congress, Monroe presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Contested Legitimacy after the 1824 Election
events.summary: Adams governed under the cloud of the “corrupt bargain” accusation, managing a legitimacy crisis without major institutional breakdown but with persistent political opposition.
sources: National Archives, 1824 election records.
Library of Congress, Adams political correspondence.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Adams faced a political legitimacy crisis he never fully dispelled, but institutions remained intact and peaceful transfer of power followed.
sources: Library of Congress, Adams presidential papers.
National Archives, early electoral crisis records.

events.title: Nullification Crisis
events.summary: Jackson confronted South Carolina’s attempt to nullify federal tariffs, threatening force, securing a Force Bill, and accepting a compromise tariff to defuse secession talk.
sources: National Archives, Jackson nullification records.
Library of Congress, Jackson crisis correspondence.

events.title: Bank War as Political‑Economic Crisis
events.summary: Jackson’s destruction of the Second Bank triggered intense financial and political turmoil, which he framed as a struggle against concentrated power.
sources: Library of Congress, Jackson bank war papers.
National Archives, financial crisis records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Jackson managed existential threats to federal authority with aggressive rhetoric and force readiness, averting immediate disunion but fueling long‑term instability.
sources: Library of Congress, Jackson presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Panic of 1837
events.summary: Van Buren faced a severe financial collapse and widespread unemployment, adhering to hard‑money principles and proposing the Independent Treasury instead of direct federal relief.
sources: National Archives, Panic of 1837 records.
Library of Congress, Van Buren economic crisis papers.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Van Buren’s crisis management prioritized financial orthodoxy over aggressive relief, deepening his political unpopularity during prolonged hardship.
sources: Library of Congress, Van Buren presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management records.

events.title: Succession Crisis Triggered by His Death
events.summary: Harrison’s death after a month in office created the first presidential succession test, resolved by Tyler asserting full presidential authority.
sources: National Archives, Harrison death and succession records.
Library of Congress, early succession correspondence.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Harrison did not manage crises himself, but his death forced a constitutional precedent on succession that shaped future crisis handling.
sources: Library of Congress, Harrison presidential papers.
National Archives, early constitutional crisis records.

events.title: Succession Legitimacy Crisis
events.summary: Tyler insisted he was fully president, not merely acting, during the first succession crisis, establishing a durable precedent for future transitions.
sources: National Archives, Tyler succession records.
Library of Congress, Tyler constitutional correspondence.

events.title: Cabinet and Party Revolt
events.summary: Tyler faced mass cabinet resignations and expulsion from the Whig Party over vetoes, managing a governance crisis with a reduced political base.
sources: Library of Congress, Tyler cabinet papers.
National Archives, executive–legislative conflict records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Tyler stabilized the succession question but governed amid persistent political crisis and party isolation.
sources: Library of Congress, Tyler presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Mexican–American War
events.summary: Polk managed a controversial war of expansion, overseeing military campaigns and domestic opposition while achieving territorial gains.
sources: National Archives, Mexican–American War records.
Library of Congress, Polk wartime correspondence.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Polk successfully executed a major war but intensified sectional tensions over slavery in the new territories.
sources: Library of Congress, Polk presidential papers.
National Archives, war crisis records.

events.title: Sectional Crisis over Slavery in the Mexican Cession
events.summary: Taylor opposed the expansion of slavery into new territories and threatened to use force against secessionists, but his death cut short his approach.
sources: National Archives, Taylor sectional crisis records.
Library of Congress, Taylor crisis correspondence.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Taylor confronted early secession talk with a hard line but did not live to see the crisis resolved.
sources: Library of Congress, Taylor presidential papers.
National Archives, sectional crisis records.

events.title: Compromise of 1850 Implementation
events.summary: Fillmore signed and enforced the Compromise of 1850, including the Fugitive Slave Act, aiming to calm sectional tensions while provoking intense Northern backlash.
sources: National Archives, Compromise of 1850 enforcement records.
Library of Congress, Fillmore crisis papers.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Fillmore temporarily eased congressional deadlock but deepened moral and political crisis over slavery enforcement.
sources: Library of Congress, Fillmore presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Bleeding Kansas
events.summary: Pierce backed pro‑slavery territorial authorities and used federal power against anti‑slavery settlers, allowing violence and chaos to escalate in Kansas.
sources: National Archives, Kansas territorial records.
Library of Congress, Pierce sectional crisis correspondence.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Pierce’s handling of the Kansas crisis inflamed sectional conflict and undermined confidence in federal neutrality.
sources: Library of Congress, Pierce presidential papers.
National Archives, domestic crisis records.

events.title: Secession Winter of 1860–1861
events.summary: Buchanan watched multiple Southern states secede while insisting he lacked constitutional authority to coerce them, leaving federal forts vulnerable and the crisis to his successor.
sources: National Archives, Buchanan secession records.
Library of Congress, Buchanan late‑term correspondence.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Buchanan’s passivity during the secession crisis is widely judged as catastrophic, failing to contain the slide toward civil war.
sources: Library of Congress, Buchanan presidential papers.
National Archives, pre‑Civil War crisis archives.

events.title: Great Depression Initial Collapse
events.summary: Hoover faced the 1929 stock market crash and ensuing economic collapse, relying on voluntary cooperation and limited federal intervention as unemployment and bank failures surged.
sources: National Archives, Hoover Depression records.
Library of Congress, Hoover crisis correspondence.

events.title: Bonus Army Confrontation
events.summary: Hoover ordered federal troops to disperse World War I veterans demanding early bonus payments, resulting in violent clashes and national outrage.
sources: Library of Congress, Bonus Army papers.
National Archives, domestic military deployment records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Hoover’s crisis management relied on limited federal action and forceful responses to unrest, deepening public dissatisfaction during economic collapse.
sources: Library of Congress, Hoover presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Banking Crisis of 1933
events.summary: FDR declared a national bank holiday and pushed emergency legislation to stabilize the financial system during widespread bank failures.
sources: National Archives, Emergency Banking Act records.
Library of Congress, FDR banking crisis papers.

events.title: World War II Homefront Mobilization
events.summary: FDR oversaw unprecedented domestic mobilization, including rationing, war production, and internal security measures.
sources: Library of Congress, WWII homefront documents.
National Archives, wartime domestic policy records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: FDR managed the Depression and WWII through sweeping federal mobilization and emergency authority.
sources: Library of Congress, Roosevelt presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Post‑War Reconversion Crisis
events.summary: Truman navigated labor strikes, inflation, and economic instability as the nation transitioned from wartime to peacetime production.
sources: National Archives, Truman reconversion records.
Library of Congress, Truman labor crisis papers.

events.title: Korean War Outbreak
events.summary: Truman committed U.S. forces to Korea under UN authority, managing a major military crisis without a formal declaration of war.
sources: Library of Congress, Truman Korean War correspondence.
National Archives, wartime crisis records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Truman confronted post‑war domestic turmoil and the sudden outbreak of the Korean War with assertive federal action.
sources: Library of Congress, Truman presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Little Rock School Desegregation Crisis
events.summary: Eisenhower deployed federal troops to enforce desegregation at Little Rock Central High School, asserting federal authority over state resistance.
sources: National Archives, Little Rock deployment records.
Library of Congress, Eisenhower civil rights papers.

events.title: Cold War Nuclear Tensions
events.summary: Eisenhower managed escalating nuclear tensions through brinkmanship and civil defense planning.
sources: Library of Congress, Eisenhower national security papers.
National Archives, Cold War crisis records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Eisenhower balanced domestic civil rights enforcement with Cold War nuclear crisis management.
sources: Library of Congress, Eisenhower presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Cuban Missile Crisis
events.summary: Kennedy confronted the Soviet deployment of nuclear missiles in Cuba, imposing a naval quarantine and negotiating withdrawal to avert nuclear war.
sources: National Archives, missile crisis records.
Library of Congress, Kennedy national security papers.

events.title: Ole Miss Integration Crisis
events.summary: Kennedy deployed federal marshals and troops to enforce the enrollment of James Meredith at the University of Mississippi.
sources: Library of Congress, Kennedy civil rights enforcement papers.
National Archives, domestic deployment records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Kennedy managed the most dangerous nuclear confrontation in history while enforcing civil rights under violent resistance.
sources: Library of Congress, Kennedy presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Vietnam War Escalation
events.summary: Johnson dramatically escalated U.S. involvement in Vietnam, managing domestic unrest and political backlash as casualties rose.
sources: National Archives, Vietnam War escalation records.
Library of Congress, Johnson wartime correspondence.

events.title: Urban Riots of the 1960s
events.summary: Johnson faced widespread urban unrest, deploying federal troops in several cities and commissioning the Kerner Report.
sources: Library of Congress, Johnson domestic crisis papers.
National Archives, riot response records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Johnson confronted simultaneous crises of war abroad and civil unrest at home, stretching federal capacity.
sources: Library of Congress, Johnson presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Watergate Scandal
events.summary: Nixon faced a constitutional crisis over the Watergate break‑in and cover‑up, resisting investigations until releasing tapes under court order.
sources: National Archives, Watergate tapes and records.
Library of Congress, Nixon crisis correspondence.

events.title: Vietnam War Drawdown
events.summary: Nixon managed the withdrawal of U.S. forces from Vietnam while expanding bombing campaigns, navigating intense domestic opposition.
sources: Library of Congress, Nixon Vietnam papers.
National Archives, wartime crisis records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Nixon’s presidency was defined by constitutional crisis and controversial wartime decision‑making.
sources: Library of Congress, Nixon presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Post‑Watergate Legitimacy Crisis
events.summary: Ford sought to stabilize the nation after Nixon’s resignation, issuing a controversial pardon to accelerate political healing.
sources: National Archives, Ford pardon records.
Library of Congress, Ford domestic crisis papers.

events.title: Fall of Saigon
events.summary: Ford managed the final evacuation of U.S. personnel and allies from Vietnam as the South Vietnamese government collapsed.
sources: Library of Congress, Ford Vietnam evacuation papers.
National Archives, crisis evacuation records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Ford’s crisis management focused on restoring trust in government and handling the chaotic end of the Vietnam War.
sources: Library of Congress, Ford presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Iran Hostage Crisis
events.summary: Carter faced a prolonged hostage crisis after militants seized the U.S. embassy in Tehran, culminating in a failed rescue attempt.
sources: National Archives, Iran hostage crisis records.
Library of Congress, Carter national security papers.

events.title: Energy Crisis
events.summary: Carter confronted fuel shortages and rising energy prices, implementing conservation measures and creating the Department of Energy.
sources: Library of Congress, Carter energy crisis papers.
National Archives, domestic crisis records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Carter’s presidency was dominated by international hostage standoff and domestic energy instability.
sources: Library of Congress, Carter presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Assassination Attempt
events.summary: Reagan survived an assassination attempt in 1981, maintaining public confidence through rapid recovery and stable leadership.
sources: National Archives, Reagan shooting records.
Library of Congress, Reagan crisis correspondence.

events.title: Iran‑Contra Affair
events.summary: Reagan faced a major scandal involving covert arms sales and unauthorized funding of Nicaraguan rebels, prompting investigations and public controversy.
sources: Library of Congress, Iran‑Contra papers.
National Archives, crisis investigation records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Reagan managed both a personal security crisis and a major constitutional scandal with mixed public reactions.
sources: Library of Congress, Reagan presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Gulf War Mobilization
events.summary: Bush assembled a broad coalition and launched Operation Desert Storm to expel Iraqi forces from Kuwait.
sources: National Archives, Gulf War records.
Library of Congress, Bush national security papers.

events.title: 1992 Los Angeles Riots
events.summary: Bush deployed federal troops to restore order during widespread unrest following the Rodney King verdict.
sources: Library of Congress, Bush domestic crisis papers.
National Archives, riot response records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Bush managed major military conflict abroad and domestic unrest at home with decisive federal action.
sources: Library of Congress, Bush presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Oklahoma City Bombing
events.summary: Clinton responded to the deadliest domestic terror attack in U.S. history with federal investigations and anti‑terrorism legislation.
sources: National Archives, Oklahoma City bombing records.
Library of Congress, Clinton crisis correspondence.

events.title: Impeachment Crisis
events.summary: Clinton faced impeachment over perjury and obstruction allegations, navigating a constitutional and political crisis while maintaining public support.
sources: Library of Congress, Clinton impeachment papers.
National Archives, constitutional crisis records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Clinton managed domestic terrorism and a constitutional crisis while maintaining governmental continuity.
sources: Library of Congress, Clinton presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: September 11 Attacks
events.summary: Bush led the federal response to the 9/11 terrorist attacks, initiating homeland security reforms and military action abroad.
sources: National Archives, 9/11 response records.
Library of Congress, Bush national security papers.

events.title: Hurricane Katrina
events.summary: Bush faced intense criticism for the federal government’s slow and disorganized response to Hurricane Katrina’s devastation.
sources: Library of Congress, Katrina response papers.
National Archives, domestic disaster records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Bush’s crisis management ranged from decisive national security action after 9/11 to widely criticized disaster response during Katrina.
sources: Library of Congress, Bush presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: Great Recession Response
events.summary: Obama implemented stimulus spending, auto industry rescue, and financial stabilization measures to address the economic crisis.
sources: National Archives, recession response records.
Library of Congress, Obama economic crisis papers.

events.title: Deepwater Horizon Oil Spill
events.summary: Obama oversaw federal response to the largest marine oil spill in U.S. history, imposing new offshore drilling regulations.
sources: Library of Congress, Deepwater Horizon papers.
National Archives, environmental crisis records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Obama managed economic collapse and major environmental disaster with large‑scale federal intervention.
sources: Library of Congress, Obama presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: COVID‑19 Pandemic Response
events.summary: Trump managed the early federal response to COVID‑19, including travel restrictions, emergency declarations, and vaccine development support.
sources: National Archives, COVID‑19 federal response records.
Library of Congress, Trump pandemic papers.

events.title: 2020 Civil Unrest
events.summary: Trump oversaw federal responses to nationwide protests and unrest following police‑involved killings, deploying federal personnel in several cities.
sources: Library of Congress, Trump domestic crisis papers.
National Archives, federal deployment records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Trump’s crisis management centered on pandemic response and domestic unrest during a polarized period.
sources: Library of Congress, Trump presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.

events.title: COVID‑19 Pandemic Continuation
events.summary: Biden expanded vaccination campaigns, federal testing programs, and economic relief measures to manage ongoing pandemic impacts.
sources: National Archives, Biden COVID‑19 response records.
Library of Congress, Biden domestic health papers.

events.title: Afghanistan Withdrawal Crisis
events.summary: Biden oversaw the rapid withdrawal of U.S. forces from Afghanistan, managing chaotic evacuations and global scrutiny.
sources: Library of Congress, Afghanistan withdrawal papers.
National Archives, crisis evacuation records.

crisisManagement.overallAssessment: Biden’s crisis management focused on pandemic stabilization and the turbulent end of the Afghanistan conflict.
sources: Library of Congress, Biden presidential papers.
National Archives, crisis management archives.
